title: Stellaris® RNA FISH FFPE (Paraffin-Embedded Tissue) Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"es6c1","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH protocol for formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1j9tp","text":"Storage Guidelines","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2nieh","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Probes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"89gb8","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Probes are shipped dry and can be stored at +2 to +8 °C in this state. Dissolved probe mix should be subjected","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"993p5","text":"to a minimum number of freeze-thaw cycles. For daily and short-term use of dissolved probe mix, storage at +2 to +8 °C in the","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9jqpf","text":"dark for up to a month is recommended. For storage lasting longer than a month, we recommend aliquoting and freezing probes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ahjso","text":"in the dark at -15 to -30 °C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e6jrf","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8tj6b","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer should be stored at +2 to +8 °C for short-term and long-term use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"70gge","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Wash Buffer A and Wash Buffer B","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"290rk","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Wash Buffers A and B should be stored at room temperature for short-term and long-term use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"lr7a","text":"Reagents and Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5g3gr","text":"Reagents and Consumables:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"evqeu","text":"a) TE buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4smpd","text":"b) 10X Phosphate Buffered Saline (PBS), RNase-free","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7iad5","text":"c) Ethanol for molecular biology","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"32oj7","text":"d) Xylene","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3eaee","text":"e) Nuclease-free water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"75u9h","text":"f) Deionized Formamide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7q4vd","text":"g) Stellaris RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer (Biosearch Technologies Cat# SMF-HB1-10)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6t233","text":"h) Stellaris RNA FISH Wash Buffer A (Biosearch Technologies Cat# SMF-WA1-60)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1ll9s","text":"i) Stellaris RNA FISH Wash Buffer B (Biosearch Technologies Cat# SMF-WB1-20)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"27p3g","text":"j) 4’,6-diamidino-2-phenylindole (DAPI)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8s112","text":"k) Vectashield® Mounting Medium (Vector Laboratories Cat #H-1000)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"akif2","text":"l) 24 mm x 60 mm, rectangular, #1 coverglass","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c2ge3","text":"m) RNase free consumables such as pipette tips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9a417","text":"n) Humidified chamber (or equivalent): 150 mm tissue culture plate; a single water-saturated paper towel placed alongside the","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"66sii","text":"inner chamber edge","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"901t5","text":"o) 37 °C laboratory oven","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5jd6e","text":"Microscope:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"73uj7","text":"a) Wide-field fluorescence microscope (e.g., Nikon Eclipse Ti or equivalent). We provide limited support for confocal applications.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2718g","text":"b) A high numerical aperture (\u003e1.3) and 60-100x oil-immersion objective.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"58kqt","text":"c) Strong light source, such as a mercury or metal-halide lamp (newer LED-based light sources may also be sufficient).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dnmd","text":"d) Filter sets appropriate for the fluorophores.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c22v3","text":"e) Standard cooled CCD camera, ideally optimized for low-light level imaging rather than speed (13 μm pixel size or less is ideal)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1n4km","text":"Preparation of Reagents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8bg2v","text":"NOTE: When performing Stellaris RNA FISH, it is imperative to limit RNA degradation. Please ensure that all consumables and","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"20cm4","text":"reagents are RNase-free. Recipes below are for set volumes. Please adjust accordingly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9jrfm","text":"Reconstituting the Dried Probe Stock","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ahmnc","text":"ShipReady Probe Set (1 nmol): A ShipReady probe set can provide up to 80 hybridizations. Re-dissolve the dried oligonucleotide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"du7cd","text":"probe blend in 80 μL of TE buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0) to create a probe stock of 12.5 μM. Mix well by pipetting","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bemg0","text":"up and down, and then vortex and centrifuge briefly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dg4lj","text":"DesignReady or Custom Probe Set (5 nmol): A DesignReady or custom probe set can provide up to 400 hybridizations.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"meci","text":"Re-dissolve the dried oligonucleotide probe blend in 400 μL of TE buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0) to create a probe","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b5as2","text":"stock of 12.5 μM. Mix well by pipetting up and down, and then vortex and centrifuge briefly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5hrpv","text":"Hybridization Buffer:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9ds6i","text":"Final composition is 10% (vol./vol.) formamide in Hybridization Buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9ac8k","text":"Hybridization Buffer should be mixed fresh for each experiment:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7mfu","text":"Due to viscosity of the solution, we recommend accounting for a 10% final volume excess in order to have enough Hybridization","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4ioce","text":"Buffer for all of your samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"86jgg","text":"For a final volume of 1 mL, mix:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"45s07","text":"900 μL Stellaris RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer (Biosearch Technologies Cat# SMF-HB1-10)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9lrd2","text":"100 μL Deionized Formamide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"br3o4","text":"NOTE: Do not freeze Hybridization Buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bvl4b","text":"WARNING! Formamide is a teratogen that is easily absorbed through the skin and should be used in a chemical fume hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bk781","text":"WARNING! Be sure to let the formamide warm to room temperature before opening the bottle.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"78jqq","text":"Wash Buffer A (150 mL):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"14mn9","text":"Final composition is 10% (vol./vol.) formamide in Wash Buffer A","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fhki7","text":"Mix and dilute Wash Buffer A fresh for each experiment:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6febt","text":"For a final volume of 150 mL, mix:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3gs0j","text":"30 mL Stellaris RNA FISH Wash Buffer A (Biosearch Technologies Cat# SMF-WA1-60)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7joq3","text":"Add 105 mL Nuclease-free water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e5i39","text":"Add 15 mL Deionized Formamide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4j5if","text":"Mix well by vortexing gently.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ak8tk","text":"Wash Buffer B:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2rodp","text":"Add Nuclease-free water to Wash Buffer B bottle upon first use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5foqj","text":"Add 88 mL of Nuclease-free water to bottle (Biosearch Technologies Cat# SMF-WB1-20) before use. Mix thoroughly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"95t95","text":"Nuclear Stain for use after hybridization:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8h363","text":"4’,6-diamidino-2-phenylindole (DAPI) dissolved in Wash Buffer A (see above) at 5 ng/mL to be used in Step I below.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9fifr","text":"Mounting media:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bhp6i","text":"The commercially available Vectashield Mounting Medium from Vector Laboratories (#H-1000).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"duhe5","text":"Note: For best results, samples mounted with Vectashield Mounting Medium should be imaged the same day.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: NOTE: The method utilized for FFPE tissue preparation, such as harvesting, fixing, and embedding, as well as the age of the FFPE tissue, are major determinants contributing to the success of Stellaris RNA FISH. Namely, care should be taken for the preservation of RNA in the sample by fixing the sample immediately upon dissection. Utilizing small samples, such as core needle biopsies, are ideal due to the rate of formalin penetration. If the level of RNA preservation in the sample is suspect, we recommend testing RNA integrity prior to performing Stellaris RNA FISH and ensuring that a proper positive control probe set is utilized simultaneously in your experiments.
Paraffin embedded tissue must be sliced at a thickness of 4-10 μm using a microtome and mounted onto a microscope slide.
Immerse the slide-mounted tissue section in 100% xylene for 10 minutes; repeat in fresh 100% xylene for an additional 5 minutes. 00:15:00
Immerse the slide in 100% ethanol for 10 minutes; repeat in fresh 100% ethanol for an additional 10 minutes. 00:10:00
Immerse the slide in 95% ethanol for 10 minutes. 00:10:00

To permeabilize the tissue section, immerse the slide in 70% ethanol for at least 1 hour at room temperature. Slides can be stored at +2 to +8 °C in 70% ethanol up to a week before hybridization. 01:00:00
Immerse the slide in 1X PBS for 2-5 minutes. 00:05:00
Decant PBS, and immerse the slide in pre-warmed (37 °C) proteinase K solution (10 μg/mL proteinase K in 1X PBS).
Incubate for 20 minutes at 37 °C. 00:20:00
Wash with 1X PBS for 2-5 minutes. 00:05:00
Wash with 1X PBS for 2-5 minutes. 00:05:00

If frozen before using, warm the reconstituted probe solution to room temperature. Mix well by vortexing, then centrifuge briefly. To prepare the Hybridization Buffer containing probe, add 2 μL of probe stock solution to 200 μL of Hybridization Buffer (enough for one coverslip), and then vortex and centrifuge. This creates a working probe solution of 125 nM. This solution will be used on steps 16 and 17. undetermined Stellaris(R) RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-HB1-10
Immerse the slide-mounted tissue section in Wash Buffer A (see recipe above) for 2-5 minutes. 00:05:00 Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer A Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WA1-60

Assemble humidified chamber: 150 mm tissue culture plate; a single water-saturated paper towel placed alongside the inner chamber edge. This chamber will help prevent evaporation of the probe solution from the tissue section.
Remove the slide from Wash Buffer A, and carefully wipe away excess buffer surrounding the tissue section.

Dispense 200 μL of Hybridization Buffer containing probe onto the tissue section of the slide. (Note that 200 μL is recommended when using a 24 mm x 60 mm, rectangular, #1 coverglass. If different sized coverglasses are used, the volume may need to be adjusted accordingly). undetermined Stellaris(R) RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-HB1-10

Carefully place a clean coverglass over the Hybridization Buffer containing probe to completely cover the tissue section, and allow for even distribution of the Hybridization Buffer. Place the slide in the humidified chamber, cover with the tissue culture lid, and seal with Parafilm®.
Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for at least 4 hours. (Incubation can be continued up to 16 hours). 04:00:00

Immerse the slide in Wash Buffer A, and allow the submerged coverglass to slide off the tissue section. Gentle agitation may be required to remove the coverglass. Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer A Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WA1-60
Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for 30 minutes. 00:30:00

Decant Wash Buffer A, and then add DAPI nuclear stain (Wash Buffer A consisting of 5 ng/mL DAPI) to counterstain the nuclei. Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer A Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WA1-60
Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for 30 minutes. 00:30:00
Decant DAPI staining buffer, and then immerse slide in Wash Buffer B for 2-5 minutes. 00:05:00 Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer B Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WB1-20
Remove the slide from Wash Buffer B, and carefully wipe away excess buffer surrounding the tissue section.

Add a small drop (approximately 50-100 μL) of Vectashield Mounting Medium onto the tissue section, and cover with a clean #1 coverglass. undetermined VECTASHIELD Mounting Medium Vector Laboratories Catalog #H-1000
Gently squeeze out excess anti-fade from underneath the coverglass.
Seal the coverglass perimeter with clear nail polish, and allow to dry.
Proceed to Imaging
